candidates.evidence_quote: The Cheesecake Factory's comparable sales were negative 2.2% in the fourth quarter, down from 0.3% in the third quarter
North Italia fourth quarter annualized AUVs totaled $7.6 million. Comparable sales declined 4%, reflecting broader industry sales trends.
Flower Child continued to perform exceptionally well and meaningfully outpaced the Fast Casual segment. Fourth quarter comparable sales increased 4%, for a two-year comp sales increase of 15%.
Other FRC sales totaled $99.4 million, up 17% from the prior year, and sales per operating week were $139,100
and external bakery sales were $17.2 million
Adjusted annualized AUVs were $12.2 million for the quarter, supported by an off-premise sales mix of 22%
supported by an off-premise sales mix of 22%, a slight improvement from recent quarters
Industry sales decelerated in the fourth quarter as reflected by the black box casual dining index declining sequentially by 410 basis points from the third quarter
and mix was a negative 1.8 and then traffic was the delta from that
Q4 pricing was about 3.5% to 4%.
We said we would see some negative mix. We did. But in both December and now again in January, we're seeing incident rates year over year growing.

Culinary innovation remains a core strength and an important differentiator for our business. The new menu items we introduced across a wide range of categories and price points continue to resonate well with guests and support our broad appeal.
Results have been encouraging, with year-over-year growth in appetizer attachment rates and improved entree ordering patterns.
working a little bit on that lunch day part because that was where we felt a little bit of the pressure over the past few quarters
We see a nice little comeback in bar incident rates at North, which is good. It's an important part of the concept and Bar mix is about 23% have been very, very stable.
Moving on to cheesecake rewards, we have made meaningful progress during the past 12 months, highlighted by strong membership growth and improved engagement.
we expect to launch a dedicated rewards app in the coming months, with the objective of creating a more seamless and connected experience for our guests
At this time, we expect to open as many as 26 new restaurants in 2026, with roughly three-quarters of those openings planned for the second half of the year
We also anticipate one to two Cheesecake Factory restaurants to open internationally under licensing agreements.
four restaurant closures that occurred toward the end of January. These closures included two cheesecake factories, one Grand Lux Cafe, and one Blanco.
continued pressure from sales transfer related to recently opened restaurants
as well as the lingering impact of the Los Angeles fires
This includes the estimated impact of inclement weather experienced so far in the quarter
We had about 1% of weather impact in Q4 and about 50 basis points of a holiday shift impact.
Again, many factors in terms of whether you want to believe it's the K economy or the government shutdown.
I think we gave some color on why we think the consumer sentiment would be soft for the fourth quarter.
we expect effective commodity inflation of low single digits for Q1, as our broad market basket remains very stable
with the remainder primarily driven by favorable commodity costs and mix shift partially offset by higher beef costs
And dairy is measurably lower. So our market basket, I think, is aiding us in that endeavor.
We are modeling net total labor inflation of low to mid single digits when factoring in the latest trends in wage rates and minimum wage increases
when factoring in the latest trends in wage rates and minimum wage increases, as well as other components of labor
driving year-over-year improvements in labor productivity, wage management, retention, and guest satisfaction
underpinned by our strong staffing position and further advancements in our industry-leading retention across both hourly staff and management
our teams drove meaningful performance improvements this quarter, including continued gains in overall guest satisfaction
The remaining difference was primarily driven by higher group medical expenses
supporting labor productivity gains and wage leverage, as well as lower payroll taxes
Total revenues were $961.6 million, inclusive of $17.3 million of gift card breakage revenue as a result of a change in historical redemption patterns
G&A, as a percent of sales, increased 70 basis points, primarily driven by the write-down of gift card inventory
Adjusted restaurant level profit margins at the Cheesecake Factory increased 60 basis points year over year to 17.6%
Excluding this benefit, fourth quarter revenues of $944.3 million finished within the range we provided
This strong operational execution supported margins and adjusted diluted net income per share, finishing toward the higher end of our expectations.
we anticipate total revenues for fiscal 2026 to be approximately $3.9 billion at the midpoint of our sensitivity modeling
we would anticipate adjusted net income margin to be about 5% at the midpoint of the sales range provided
Pre-opening costs were $9.4 million in the quarter compared to $7.6 million in the prior year period.
partially offset by timing of marketing spend
G&A is estimated to be approximately $63 million to $64 million.
Depreciation is expected to be about $115 million for the year.
we announced an increase to our share repurchase authorization and raised our quarterly dividend for the first quarter
we announced an increase to our share repurchase authorization and raised our quarterly dividend for the first quarter
we would anticipate approximately $210 million in cash capex to support unit development, as well as required maintenance on our restaurants
we recorded a pre-tax net expense of $24.6 million related to impairment of assets and lease termination expenses
we recorded a pre-tax net expense of $24.6 million related to impairment of assets and lease termination expenses
We are assuming a tax rate of approximately 5% to 6% due to the timing of certain discrete items in the quarter
as well as periodic true-ups related to stock-based compensation

notes: Chunk contains a single event: CAKE Q4 2025 earnings call transcript (part 1 of 2); names coined only from the visible part. No fiscal_kpis in this chunk (those appear only on chunk_001).
All events reviewed in order (1 of 1).

Skipped as vague/not source-claimed causes (10): 'K economy' (metaphor); tax refunds (management explicitly does not count on them); gas prices (management states no correlation); 'consumer spending backdrop' (analyst question phrasing only); convertible notes/debt balance recitation (no causal claim); liquidity/cash balance recitation; utilities timing (too minor/vague); FRC acquisition-related items (folded into asset_impairment/lease_termination quote); weighted average shares modeling assumption; 'wage management' (folded into labor_inflation/labor_productivity).

Gray-zone flags: frc_sales uses the company's own segment label FRC (Fox Restaurant Concepts portfolio), treated as an allowed segment/brand name like north_italia; eps_surprise and revenue_surprise are reported-vs-company-expected-range (no consensus mentioned in this part); bar_sales_mix and lunch_daypart_sales evidence is attributed to the North Italia concept but named at the metric level.
